current_pattern: Fragmented: multiple point solutions per brand, Excel-based tools, limited API integration
target_pattern: Unified Azure-native lending platform: single serviceability engine, shared origination API, Pega workflow across both brands, broker-facing portal as first-class channel
target_principles: API-first; Azure-native; Approved components only; Single source of truth per data domain
rationale: Fragmented lending estate increases credit policy risk and broker experience friction. Unified platform reduces maintenance overhead and enables consistent policy enforcement.

current_pattern: Multiple on-premises data warehouses by brand. Limited cross-brand analytics. No ML capability in production.
target_pattern: Snowflake Enterprise Data Hub as single analytical layer. Azure Data Factory for ingestion. Power BI as enterprise reporting standard. ML workbench for predictive models.
target_principles: Cloud-native; Domain-oriented data ownership; AI-ready data foundations
rationale: Fragmented data estate limits analytics maturity and AI/ML adoption. Unified data hub enables cross-brand insights, regulatory reporting, and ML model development.

current_pattern: Oracle Siebel CRM (Coastal brand) plus fragmented Summit CRM tooling. No unified customer view. Limited marketing automation.
target_pattern: Salesforce CRM as single customer record across both brands. Service Cloud for contact centre. Marketing Cloud for campaigns. MuleSoft integration to core banking.
target_principles: SaaS-first; Single customer view; API-first integration
rationale: Siebel is end-of-life and inhibits customer experience investment. Salesforce provides modern CRM capability with AI features and native integration.

current_pattern: Rule-based fraud detection. Manual AML monitoring. Compliance coverage tracked in spreadsheets.
target_pattern: Azure ML-based fraud detection. Automated AML with graph analytics. Compliance programme tracked in real-time via Architecture as Data platform.
rationale: Rule-based risk systems are falling behind sophisticated fraud patterns. Compliance monitoring requires continuous oversight that manual processes cannot sustain.

current_pattern: Separate brand digital channels with independent tech stacks. No shared component library. Broker portal built on Siebel.
target_pattern: Shared Azure-native digital platform. Micro-frontend architecture with shared design system. Unified identity via Entra External ID. Modern broker portal as independent Azure SPA.
target_principles: API-first; Azure-native; Shared components; Design system
rationale: Fragmented digital channels increase cost, slow feature delivery, and create inconsistent customer experiences across Coastal and Summit brands.
